methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Blitzpanel is a pre-seed stage robotics and manufacturing company operating within a sector represented by 9 identified comparable companies: 1 public entity (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The company's total raised and current valuation remain undisclosed, and founder identity is under wraps.

Peer-median valuation methodology was not applicable to this analysis. The robotics and manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector comparables, but none satisfied the necessary filtering criteria for narrow peer-set triangulation—specifically, zero companies matched on both stage and adjacent-stage metrics simultaneously, and zero fell within a similar valuation tier. Standard practice requires a minimum of three datapoints to establish a defensible peer median; the absence of same-stage or tightly clustered private precedents in this niche precludes reliance on this method without introducing material noise.

Given the methodological constraints, no triangulated valuation range can be supported by the supplied dataset. A meaningful analysis would require either (1) expansion to adjacent-stage robotics companies with disclosed valuations, (2) application of alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture capital method using company-specific financial assumptions, or (3) direct precedent transactions in narrow robotics/manufacturing pre-seed cohorts. The stealth nature of founding team and undisclosed fundraising history further limits conventional benchmarking approaches.
